phase-scope.phase-37: Live MTProto edge runtime fix for clean-VPS Telegram proxy unavailability; local verification passed, but RU mtproto_proxy downstream handshakes were superseded by the DE-backed runtime path.
phase-scope.phase-38: Phase-38 keeps reusable runtime and policy bridge artifacts but replaces RU-only live traffic ownership with a DE-backed runtime behind an RU SNI router.
phase-scope.phase-41: Phase-41 reuses M-049 as the production KPprotoN fake-TLS data-plane on DE, updates the locked upstream proxy, enforces a non-empty ad tag, and has operator Telegram Desktop/mobile proof.

commands.command-2: rm -rf /tmp/krotpn-mtproto-runtime-check && rsync -a --exclude='_build' --exclude='rebar3.crashdump' mtproto-runtime/ /tmp/krotpn-mtproto-runtime-check/ && cd /tmp/krotpn-mtproto-runtime-check && rebar3 as prod release
commands.command-3: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_mtproto_api.py -v
commands.command-5: node scripts/phase32-edge-contract-smoke.mjs && node scripts/phase34-mtproto-stabilization-smoke.mjs && node scripts/phase35-installer-wildcard-tls-smoke.mjs && node scripts/phase37-mtproto-runtime-smoke.mjs
commands.command-6: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-37.xml docs/modules/M-049.xml docs/verification/V-M-049.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml && mygrace lint
commands.command-7: operator-live: RU/DE edge health, port ownership, HTTPS fallback, existing active SNI replay, and Telegram Desktop/mobile available status passed after clean deploy.
commands.command-8: live: mtp_ping req_pq probe passed against one issued fake-TLS link on DC +1 through +5 after DE runtime rebuild and non-empty PROXY_AD_TAG wiring.

verification-levels.module-local: Erlang compile/release, backend HTTP adapter pytest, provisioning live-apply pytest, static runtime smoke, bash syntax, compose config, XML parse, and MyGRACE lint.
verification-levels.wave-level: M-044, M-046, and M-048 checks must pass together because runtime policy, port ownership, and deploy env are a single production behavior.
verification-levels.phase-level: RU/DE smoke proves the SNI router and DE mtproto-edge route are healthy, issued SNI is loaded into live policy, issued-link mtp_ping passes, and Telegram client proof is operator-confirmed.

module-checks.check-1: mtproto-runtime builds and exposes token-protected apply, revoke, and health routes.
module-checks.check-2: docker-compose defines mtproto-edge in host network mode with PROXY_PORT=443, cert mounts, policy token, and domain-fronting fallback.
module-checks.check-3: RU nginx no longer binds public 443 and instead serves the HTTPS fallback on private 9443 while admin remains public 8443.
module-checks.check-4: deploy writes MTPROTO_RUNTIME_POLICY_URL, MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN, MTPROTO_POLICY_PORT, EDGE_SHARED_443_ENABLED, and EDGE_HTTPS_FALLBACK_PORT.
module-checks.check-5: backend HTTP adapter posts apply/revoke and gets health with the token header while redacting token and proxy secrets.
module-checks.check-6: provisioning applies the SNI to runtime before returning an active user payload.
module-checks.check-7: Telegram core bootstrap fetches are bounded and static fallback artifacts are returned when core.telegram.org is slow, blocked, yields no downstreams, or yields a filtered config missing common Telegram DC pools.
module-checks.check-8: Deploy persists Telegram MTProto upstream routes through awg-client for 149.154.160.0/20, 91.108.4.0/22, 91.108.56.0/22, and 91.105.192.0/23.
module-checks.check-9: RU server has mtproto-edge healthy, TCP 443 owned by the edge, TCP 8443 owned by nginx fallback, and policy_count includes issued active SNI after replay.
module-checks.check-10: Telegram Desktop/mobile mark the issued proxy available from the existing user dashboard link.
module-checks.check-11: Phase-38 must not delete Phase-37 runtime artifacts; it reuses policy API and runtime code while moving production traffic to DE.
module-checks.check-12: DE KPprotoN runtime accepts per-SNI fake-TLS policy and does not require official MTProxy secret manifests for user issuance.
module-checks.check-13: mtproto_proxy is locked to commit 154a8fb8e6ef85460f502d4b74434a22a5836937 with modern fake-TLS ping/backpressure fixes needed for live req_pq diagnostics.
module-checks.check-14: DE KPprotoN runtime uses non-empty PROXY_AD_TAG; zero tag is accepted and live mtp_ping proves Telegram proxy_req responses are returned.
module-checks.check-15: Runtime entrypoint waits for POLICY_LISTEN_IP before release boot, and policy apply replaces an existing SNI before adding it again.
module-checks.check-16: Runtime fallback proxy config includes DC +/-1, +/-2, +/-3, +/-4, +/-5, and +/-203 so mtproto_proxy cannot remain nominally healthy while real clients fail with no_pool for a common Telegram DC.

scenarios.scenario-1: Fresh deploy starts mtproto-edge with wildcard TLS and no public 443 conflict.
scenarios.scenario-2: Existing active assignment SNI is applied to the live policy table without changing its secret or link.
scenarios.scenario-3: MTProto bootstrap returns local fallback secret/config when Telegram core is unavailable or filtered bootstrap is incomplete, allowing mtproto_proxy to bind 443 with common DC pools.
scenarios.scenario-4: Normal https://krotpn.xyz traffic is forwarded to nginx fallback and still reaches frontend, API, and admin surfaces.
scenarios.scenario-5: Missing runtime token, unreachable policy API, invalid SNI, or policy API auth failure returns degraded state and never claims an active usable proxy.
scenarios.scenario-6: Port bind conflict or broken fallback requires rollback to previous nginx-only edge before user traffic is advertised.
scenarios.scenario-7: Phase-38 reuses runtime policy semantics but no longer requires RU mtproto_proxy downstream handshakes for production Telegram availability.
scenarios.scenario-8: Phase-41 DE runtime applies a new issued SNI without restarting the data-plane process.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Never print MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN, MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX, MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT, derived fake-TLS secrets, full tg://proxy links, private keys, JWTs, refresh tokens, Resend API keys, or email verification tokens.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Live evidence may include service names, port ownership roles, status codes, aggregate policy_count, and redacted SNI suffix only.

live-smoke-gates.gate-1: docker ps shows mtproto-edge and backend healthy.
live-smoke-gates.gate-2: RU ss output shows public TCP 443 owned by the SNI router and nginx HTTPS fallback on 9443; DE ss output shows mtproto-edge/beam owns TCP 443.
live-smoke-gates.gate-3: Local/private policy health succeeds only with MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN.
live-smoke-gates.gate-4: MTProto bootstrap secret/config endpoints respond within the configured proxy startup timeout.
live-smoke-gates.gate-5: Existing active assignment SNI apply returns success through the local policy API or scheduler replay.
live-smoke-gates.gate-6: curl https://krotpn.xyz returns a healthy frontend response without TLS bypass.
live-smoke-gates.gate-7: Operator Telegram Desktop/mobile retest marks the proxy available and usable.
live-smoke-gates.gate-8: Phase-38 handoff verifies M-050 owns the replacement live gates before M-049 is considered superseded in production.
live-smoke-gates.gate-9: Phase-41 handoff verifies M-049 owns the KPprotoN fake-TLS runtime after official MTProxy is disabled from production.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: Compile, release, pytest, bash syntax, static smoke, compose config, XML parse, and MyGRACE lint.
autonomous-execution-safety.requires-operator: Future production RU/DE restarts, public 443 ownership changes, and Telegram Desktop/mobile availability proof for release-impacting changes.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-18: Local diagnosis confirmed issued assignment existed but no live MTProto runtime process owned public 443.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-18: Live RU first run showed policy health but no TCP 443 bind because core bootstrap timed out; bounded fallback bootstrap is now in scope.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-18: Telegram upstream routes through awg-client restored TCP reachability, but mtproto_proxy downstream handshakes still closed; nginx public 443 rollback preserved web availability.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 verification moves live availability proof to V-M-050 while preserving Phase-37 artifacts as reusable runtime components.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 local execution keeps mtproto-runtime buildable and policy-handler tests intact while default compose no longer starts RU mtproto-edge as the public 443 owner.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 verification design restores M-049 as the DE KPprotoN fake-TLS runtime and requires comparison against CodexKPprotoN_for_KPN behavior.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 local execution passed rebar3 compile and static smoke proving the active production path uses KPprotoN runtime/policy semantics, with live Telegram proof still operator-required.
evidence.evidence-8: 2026-05-19: Live diagnosis showed assignment policy, public RU route, fake-TLS accept, and Telegram downstream nonce probe passed, but issued-link req_pq timed out on the old e343e30 mtproto_proxy lock. The fix updates the dependency lock to 154a8fb for rebuild and live retest.
evidence.evidence-9: 2026-05-19: Live diagnosis after the upstream lock update proved the remaining blocker was blank PROXY_AD_TAG. With zero 32-hex tag, scheduler replay applied assignment_id=1 and mtp_ping for the issued fake-TLS link returned OK on DC +1 through +5.
evidence.evidence-10: 2026-05-19: Operator clean-deploy validation confirmed VPN and Telegram Desktop/mobile MTProto proxy work from the Phase-41 issued link.
evidence.evidence-11: 2026-05-23: Local smoke and static pytest cover POLICY_LISTEN_IP wait and idempotent SNI replace-before-add after live reboot diagnosis.
evidence.evidence-12: 2026-05-23: Live promo-tag/reboot diagnosis found no_pool,2 in DE runtime logs; kpproton_core_api now requires complete common Telegram DC pools and uses an expanded static fallback plus a 1000ms downstream probe timeout. The current DE runtime was rebuilt and redacted Phase-39 live smoke passed fake-TLS accept with Telegram downstream proxy_for_count=24.
evidence.evidence-13: 2026-05-24: Operator clean-deployed the release candidate from GitHub, rebooted both RU/DE hosts, and confirmed issued KPprotoN fake-TLS proxies recovered and worked after the normal startup delay. Complete Telegram downstream fallback is accepted as the stable production runtime path.
